# Built Young

> A live, hands-on money program for teenagers (ages 13–18). Tagline: "Raising builders, not consumers." Students learn money by living it — earning a simulated paycheck, investing, budgeting, managing credit, making big purchases, and building net worth.

## About
Built Young is an online financial-education program founded by Sunil Garg. It runs as a 12-week course (90-minute weekly sessions) plus 6 monthly check-ins, delivered live over Zoom. Each class, students earn a simulated $1,000 paycheck and make real-world money decisions inside an interactive simulation. The program is financial education, not licensed financial advice; all money in the simulation is simulated.

## Who it's for
Teens ages 13 and up, in two tracks:
- Middle School cohort — $599
- High School cohort — $799 (includes a "Paying for College" unit on financial aid and student loans)

## What students learn
- Paychecks, taxes, and 401(k) basics
- Saving, investing, diversification, and compounding
- How macro forces (inflation, interest rates, recessions) move markets
- Big purchases: buying and financing a home and a car; good vs. bad debt
- Budgeting, credit and credit scores
- Building and protecting wealth (including alternatives like private equity) and avoiding scams
- Earning in an AI world — being a builder, not just a consumer

## Enrolling
Families can book a free 15-minute call with the founder before enrolling. A prorated refund is available through the end of Act 1 (first 3 weeks).

## Contact
